title: toxicodendron horridum
intro: Toxicodendron horridum, also known as poison oak or poison ivy, is a plant species that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family and is characterized by its shiny leaves with lobed or toothed edges. The plant contains a toxic sap that can cause severe skin irritation, but it has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Toxicodendron horridum include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and swelling, as well as promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make topical creams, salves, and ointments that can be applied directly to the affected area to alleviate symptoms.
